rationale: The citing paper’s core constructs and claims (conceptual/perceptual priming, processing fluency, and effects on brand/product evaluation) closely match the cited paper’s central contribution on conceptual and perceptual fluency effects in brand evaluation. The citing note explicitly says it extends prior conceptual-fluency/priming work, making this cited 2004 JMR article a plausible foundational theory/mechanism source rather than an incidental mention.
